rulings.Case Title: AVI EDRI VS. TAMAR RACHEL KASSOFF-SOFIOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Ellis Ros Anderson; Declaration Of Michael D. Kolodzi
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 01, 2026. Line 1. PLAINTIFF AVI EDRI , AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6; is continued to July 13, 2026 per stipulation. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GORDON STARR ET AL VS. NORMAN ONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 01, 2026. Line 2. DEFENDANT NORMAN ONG, EILEEN ONG, 2030 STEINER STREET CONDOMINIUM PROJECT DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Parties are ordered to meet and confer as required by CCP 430.41 "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" no later than July 17, 2026. If parties do not agree on a mutually acceptable date and manner for the conference, the parties shall meet and confer in person at 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco, CA at 1:30 p.m. on July 9, 2026. Thereafter, if necessary, Defendant may file a demurrer accompanied by a declaration attesting to "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" meet and confer conference. Defendant shall not be charged a fee. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CP CLEMENT, LLC VS. DAVID SILVERMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 01, 2026. Line 13. DEFENDANT DAVID SILVERMAN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. This Court has jurisdiction of the subject of the cause of action alleged in the pleading, and the Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161(1), which specifically includes termination of a licensor-licensee relationship as a basis for unlawful detainer. Defendant does not identify any requirement of under this subsection with which Plaintiff's Complaint fails to sufficiently allege compliance. Defendant's objection to Plaintiff's opposition based on untimeliness is overruled. The opposition was timely filed pursuant to CCP 1170(c). Defendant acknowledges receipt of the opposition and filed a reply. The Court notes that this demurrer challenges only the factual allegations stated on the face of the Complaint. The Court therefore does not consider any extrinsic evidence, including any proffered or requested declarations or testimony, in connection with this demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
